evidences.title: 2025 Formula 1 calendar announced
evidences.content: Azerbaijan hosts Round 17 on 19-21 September, followed by the Singapore Grand Prix on 3-5 October. Austin hosts the US Grand Prix on 17-19 October ahead of the Mexico City Grand Prix on 24-26 October. The São Paulo Grand Prix is the final standalone race of 2025 on 7-9 November, before the season ends with a triple header of Las Vegas (20-22 November), Qatar (28-30 November) and Abu Dhabi (5-7 December). ## REPRESENT THETEAM ## RELATED ARTICLES FORMULA 1 ### 2024 Autosport Awards winners revealed ### 2025 F1 Academy calendar announced ### F1 announce inaugural all-team season launch for 2025 ### Ella Lloyd to represent McLaren for the 2025 F1 Academy season ### Formula 1 announce the six Sprint venues for 2025 ## McLAREN MASTERCARD FORMULA 1 PARTNERS [...] Following the season’s second triple header, we race in Montreal, Canada, on 13-15 June before returning to Europe on 27-29 June for the Austrian Grand Prix, which is followed by ours and Lando’s home race, the British Grand Prix at Silverstone on 4-5 July. Ahead of the summer break, the Belgian Grand Prix takes place on 25-27 July, followed by the Hungarian Grand Prix on 1-3 August. We return to action following F1’s summer h...

evidences.title: 2025 FIA Formula One World Championship calendar ...
evidences.content: Formula 1 CEO and President Stefano Domenicali commented, “2025 will be a special year as we celebrate the 75th Anniversary of the FIA Formula One World Championship, and it’s that legacy and experience that allows us to deliver such a strong calendar. Once again, we’ll visit 24 incredible venues around the world, delivering top-class racing, hospitality, and entertainment, which will be enjoyed by millions of fans worldwide.” The season kicks off with the Australian Grand Prix from March 14-16 and concludes with the Abu Dhabi Grand Prix on December 5-7. The schedule spans five continents, promising a diverse and exciting series of races. With Ramadan falling in March, the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix have been rescheduled for April to accommodate cultural observances.
